rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE P LIN VS. PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, ACTING BY AND et al

Overruled as to all claims that are the subject of the demurrer. The claims are neither time-barred nor precluded by res judicata. rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Deft Folsom/First Llc'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records, Issued To John Muir Medical Center (Re Pltf David Yang); For Protective Order; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 21, 2011, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS DAVID YANG AND MIMI YANG'S Notice And Motion To Quash Defendantt Folsom/First Llc'S Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records, Issued To John Muir Medical Center (Re Pltf David Yang); For Protective Order; And For Sanctions. Granted. The subpoena is not narrowly tailored to the issues in dispute and the very significant overbreadth impinges on Mr. Yang¿s privacy. Sanctions denied. Defendant¿s position did not lack substantial justification due to Mr. Yang¿s somewhat vague request for annoyance and discomfort damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST VS. NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Request For Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arb Award;Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 21, 2011, Line 10, PETITIONER TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST Notice Of Request For Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award; Request For Judicial Notice. The Court confirms the arbitration award. Judgment in favor of petitioner shall be entered against Paragon. The Court dismisses NAC pursuant to CCP 1287.2. NAC is not bound by the award and was not a party to the arbitration. NAC was vindicating the rights of its insured. Petitioner is not barred from pursuing an 11580 action against NAC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
